Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>1. цЬЙхБЪ</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-06-22</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

тЬЕ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

тЭМ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
хЕищГишнЙцУЪхИ░щ╜КуАВщЕНх░НцШп `kiro-cli acp`уАВшйХф╝░ч╡РшлЦш╖ЯцИСчммф╕Аш╝кшмЫчЪДф╕Нф╕Ацигф║Ж тАФтАФ **цЦ╣цбИ A ч┤Ф index х░Нф╜ачЪД steering ф╕НхдачФи,шАМф╕ФхПпшГ╜ца╣цЬмцШпхдЪщдШчЪД**,хПЦц▒║цЦ╝ф╕АхАЛщВДц▓ТщйЧшнЙчЪДф║ЛхпжуАВцИСцККхоГшмЫц╕ЕцеЪуАВ

## хЕИшмЫцЬАщЧЬщН╡чЪД:цЬЙхАЛхЕНш▓╗ц╕мшйжшГ╜ц▒║хоЪуАМшжБф╕НшжБхБЪуАН

ф╜ачЪД `task-acknowledgement.md` цШпф╕АцвЭ **always-on шбМчВ║шжПхЙЗ**:

```
# ф╗╗хЛЩчв║шкНца╝х╝П
цФ╢хИ░ф╗╗ф╜Хф╗╗хЛЩцЩВя╝МхЬищЦЛхзЛхЯ╖шбМхЙНх┐ЕщаИхЕИш╝╕хЗ║я╝Ъ
ЁЯЯа цФ╢хИ░я╝Ъ[...] / ЁЯЯа цЙУчоЧя╝Ъ[...]
тЪая╕П цЙАцЬЙф╗╗хЛЩф╕Ах╛Лшз╕чЩ╝я╝МчДбф╛ЛхдЦ
```

шАМ sync.ps1 ши╗шзгшккуАМKiro щЦЛцйЯшЗкхЛХш╝ЙхЕе `~/.kiro/steering/*.md`уАНуАВхХПщбМцШп:**`kiro-cli acp`(headless цибх╝П,швл bridge spawn)хИ░х║ХцЬЙц▓ТцЬЙш╝Й steering?** щАЩцИСх╛Ю repo чДбц│Хчв║шкН тАФтАФ ф╜Жф╜ацпПхдйчФих╛ЧхИ░чЪД Telegram bot х░▒цШпчнФцбИ:

> **ф╜ачЪД Telegram bot чП╛хЬихЫЮф╗╗хЛЩцЩВ,цЬГф╕НцЬГхЕИш╝╕хЗ║уАМЁЯЯа цФ╢хИ░ / ЁЯЯа цЙУчоЧуАН?**
> - **цЬГ** тЖТ Kiro ACP х╖▓ч╢УцЬЙш╝Й steering тЖТ bridge щАЩцвЭш╖пхЕ╢хпжчЬЛх╛ЧхИ░ steering тЖТ **щАЩхАЛуАМцЦ╖щ╗ЮуАНца╣цЬмф╕НхнШхЬи(шЗ│х░Сх░Н Kiro щЕНх░Н),цЦ╣цбИ A цШпхдЪщдШчЪД**,хПкхЙйуАМхИЗхИ░ Codex/Claude цЩВшбМчВ║ф╕АшЗ┤уАНчЪДцмбшжБхГ╣хА╝уАВ
> - **ф╕НцЬГ** тЖТ цЦ╖щ╗ЮцШпчЬЯчЪД,steering х░Н bridge agent чв║хпжчДбцХИ тЖТ цЙНхА╝х╛ЧхБЪуАВ

щАЩф╕АхАЛшзАхпЯх░▒шГ╜чЬБф╕ЛцХ┤хАЛхпжф╜ЬшйХф╝░уАВх╗║шн░ф╜ахЕИхЫЮчнФщАЩхАЛуАВ

---

ф╗еф╕ЛхБЗшинуАМф╕НцЬГуАН(цЦ╖щ╗ЮчВ║чЬЯ),шйХф╝░цЦ╣цбИ A цЬмш║л:

## цЦ╣цбИ A ч┤Ф index **ф╕Нхда** тАФтАФ хЫачВ║ steering цШпц╖╖хРИхЮЛшкЮцЦЩ

цИСчЬЛф║Жф╕Йф╗╜цкФчЪДхпжщЪЫхЕзхо╣,хоГхАСхИЖх▒мхЕйчиохоМхЕиф╕НхРМчЪДц│ихЕещЬАц▒В:

| цкФцбИ | хдзх░П | хЮЛхИе | цЦ╣цбИ A(index)щБйчФи? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `task-acknowledgement.md` | 354B | **always-on шбМчВ║**(цпПхАЛф╗╗хЛЩщГ╜шжБ ЁЯЯа) | тЭМ ф╕НшбМ |
| `closed-loop-system.md` | 4.9KB | **always-on ч│╗ч╡▒цПРчд║**(ч╣Бф╕н+цЦ╣ц│ХшлЦ trade-off) | тЪая╕П щГихИЖ(always-on ф╜Жх╛Ихдз) |
| `skill-workflow.md` | 1.7KB | **чЫ╕щЧЬцЩВцЙНцЯе**(хПкхЬихЛХ skill цЩВцЬЙчФи) | тЬЕ хоМч╛О |

цЦ╣цбИ A чЪДцЬмш│кцШпуАМхИЧч┤вх╝Х,agent шж║х╛ЧчЫ╕щЧЬцЙН `read`уАНуАВф╜Ж `task-acknowledgement` щАЩчиоуАМчДбф╛ЛхдЦуАБцпПцмбщГ╜шжБшз╕чЩ╝уАНчЪДшжПхЙЗ,**ф╕НшГ╜ф║дч╡ж agent шЗкчФ▒шгБщЗП** тАФтАФ ф╕НцФ╛щА▓ prefix хоГх░▒ф╕НцЬГ fireуАВцЙАф╗ецЦ╣цбИ A х░НхоГца╣цЬмчДбцХИуАВ`skill-workflow` хПНшАМх╛ИщБйхРИ index(хПкцЬЙцФ╣ skill цЩВцЙНщЬАшжБ)уАВ

**ч╡РшлЦ:ф╕Ншй▓ч╡▒ф╕АчФицЦ╣цбИ A,шй▓чФиуАМхИЖщбЮц│ихЕеуАН(hybrid)уАВ**

## х╗║шн░чЪДшиншиИ:frontmatter щйЕхЛХчЪД hybrid

```
inject: always  тЖТ хЕицЦЗхбЮщА▓ preamble(task-acknowledgementуАБхПпшГ╜ closed-loop чЪДца╕х┐Г)
inject: index   тЖТ хПкхИЧциЩщбМ+ш╖пх╛С,хПл agent цМЙщЬА read(skill-workflow)
(чДб frontmatter) тЖТ щаРшин index(хоЙхЕиф┐ЭхоИ)
```

хпжф╜Ьх░▒цШпцКК `buildWikiIndexBlock()`(`memory.ts:699-720`)цУ┤цИР `buildSteeringBlock()`:

```
function buildSteeringBlock(): string {
  const dir = path.join(detectAgentConfigDir(), "steering");   // ц│ицДП:хЬи AGENT_CONFIG_DIR ф╕НцШп MEMORY_DIR
  // шоА *.md тЖТ шзгцЮР frontmatter inject цмД
  //   always тЖТ хЕицЦЗ + [Steering policy: <title>] ... [End]
  //   index  тЖТ "- <title> тАФ ${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR}/steering/<file>(read х╛МхЖНхБЪчЫ╕щЧЬх╖еф╜Ь)"
}
```

цХ┤хРИщ╗Ю(щГ╜х╖▓хоЪф╜Н):
- `buildPreambleSections()` хКахЫЮхВ│ `steeringBlock`
- `buildPreambleWithBreakdown()` цЦЗхнЧф╕▓цОе(`memory.ts:315-335`)+ `PreambleBreakdown` ф╗ЛщЭвхКа `steeringBlock` цмДф╜Н(`memory.ts:258-279`,ш╖Я `wikiBlock` хРМч┤Ъ)
- ш╖пх╛СчФи `${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR}/steering/<file>` placeholder(хПпцФЬ,ш╖и Kiro/Codex/Claude),ц▓┐чФи wiki чФи `${MEMORY_DIR}` чЪДцЕгф╛Л

## ч┤ДцЭЯцквцЯе

- **Frozen-snapshot тЬЕ**:щАЩхбКш╖Я wikiBlock ф╕Ациг,session х╗║члЛцЩВ build ф╕АцмбуАБхЗНч╡Р,ф╕НщБХхПН invariantуАВф╜Ж**цЬГхЛХхИ░ `buildPreamble*`,цЙАф╗ешжБщБО `preamble-frozen-snapshot.md` чЪД change-this-policy checklist**(чв║шкНф╕НхЬищЭЮщжЦ turn щЗНчЩ╝уАБgrep чДбцЦ░ caller)тАФтАФ щЦАцк╗ф╜О,чЕзхБЪхН│хПпуАВ
- **Token цИРцЬм**:full-inject `task-ack`(~90 tok)+ index хЕйцФп(~60 tok)тЙИ **150 tok,ф╕ФщжЦ turn х╛МщА▓ prefix cache**,хПпх┐╜чХеуАВшЛе `closed-loop` ф╣ЯхЕицЦЗхбЮ тЖТ +~1200 tok(щАЩцШпхФпф╕АшжБф╜ацЛНцЭ┐чЪДцИРцЬмц▒║чнЦ:4.9KB цЦ╣ц│ХшлЦшжБф╕НшжБцпП session х╕╕щзР)уАВ
- **Windows-shell-safety тЬЕ**:чФи `readdirSync`/`readFileSync`(native fs),ф╕Нш╡░ PowerShellуАВ
- **хЫЮхблцнгцЬм**:шжБхКа `inject:` frontmatter хИ░ canonical чЪД steering цкФ тЖТ цФ╣цнгцЬм тЖТ `sync.ps1 -Apply`уАВ

## х╖ещаЕшИЗцИРцЬм

~40-55 шбМцЦ░хЗ╜х╝П + ~6 шбМ wiring + 1 хАЛ breakdown цмДф╜Н + 1 цФп smoke test(`check-steering-block.mjs`)+ canonical frontmatterуАВщЫЖф╕нхЬи `memory.ts` хЦоцкФуАВщаРф╝░ **>2k tokens чФвхЗ║**,ф╛Эф╜ачЪД `vc-kiro-delegate` цЕгф╛ЛщБйхРИф╕Яч╡ж Kiro хпжф╜ЬуАВ

## цИСчЪДх╗║шн░щаЖх║П

1. **хЕИхЫЮчнФщВгхАЛхЕНш▓╗ц╕мшйж**(Telegram bot чП╛хЬицЬГф╕НцЬГ ЁЯЯацФ╢хИ░/ЁЯЯацЙУчоЧ)уАВцЬГ тЖТ хдЪхНКф╕НчФихБЪуАВ
2. шЛешжБхБЪ тЖТ чФи **hybrid** шАМщЭЮч┤ФцЦ╣цбИ A,ф╕жхЕИц▒║хоЪ `closed-loop-system` шжБ always щВДцШп index(цИРцЬмх╖о ~1200 tok/session)уАВ
3. цЛНцЭ┐х╛МцИСщЦЛ SPEC + хзФц┤╛ Kiro хпжф╜ЬуАВ

шжБф╕НшжБцИСхЕИчнЙф╜ащВгхАЛшзАхпЯч╡РцЮЬ?

<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>

  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness тАФ fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly тАФ if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; highтЖТmax: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only тАФ it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). The user is asking me to generate a progress summary of what was done in the primary Claude session. Let me analyze what happened:

The user sent "1. цЬЙхБЪ" (meaning "1. Have done" in Chinese), and Claude provided a detailed Chinese response about evaluating steering file integration approaches for a Kiro-CLI system.

Let me break down what Claude discussed:

1. **Context**: Evaluating how to integrate steering files (task-acknowledgement.md, closed-loop-system.md, skill-workflow.md) into the Kiro-CLI agent configuration system, specifically for the `kiro-cli acp` (bridge mode) scenario.

2. **Key Discovery**: Claude identified a critical test that needs to be done first - checking whether the Telegram bot currently outputs "ЁЯЯа цФ╢хИ░ / ЁЯЯа цЙУчоЧ" (acknowledgment format) when receiving tasks. This would reveal if steering files are already being loaded in headless mode.

3. **Analysis of Approach A (pure index)**: Claude evaluated whether a pure index-based approach would work and concluded it's insufficient because:
   - task-acknowledgement.md is an "always-on" behavior rule that must fire every time
   - Index approach relies on agent discretion to read files, which won't work for mandatory rules
   - Different steering files have different injection needs (always-on vs. contextual)

4. **Proposed Solution**: A hybrid approach using frontmatter-driven injection:
   - `inject: always` тЖТ Full text in preamble
   - `inject: index` тЖТ Listed in index, read on demand
   - Implementation would extend `buildWikiIndexBlock()` into `buildSteeringBlock()`

5. **Implementation Details**: 
   - Modify `buildPreambleSections()` and `buildPreambleWithBreakdown()`
   - Add steering block to PreambleBreakdown interface
   - Token cost: ~150 tokens (or ~1350 if closed-loop is fully injected)
   - Estimated ~40-55 lines of new code

6. **Next Steps**: Claude suggested waiting for the user to answer the critical test question about whether the Telegram bot currently shows acknowledgment format.

This is an architectural investigation and design discussion, not actual implementation work yet.

content.text:
<summary>
  <request>Evaluate steering file integration approach for Kiro-CLI bridge mode and determine if pure index-based injection is sufficient</request>
  <investigated>Claude analyzed three steering files (task-acknowledgement.md, closed-loop-system.md, skill-workflow.md) to determine their injection requirements and evaluated whether Approach A (pure index-based loading) would work for the kiro-cli acp bridge scenario. Examined the nature of each file's content and usage patterns to classify injection needs.</investigated>
  <learned>Steering files have fundamentally different injection requirements: task-acknowledgement.md is an always-on behavior rule that must trigger for every task (cannot rely on agent discretion), while skill-workflow.md is contextual (only needed when modifying skills). A pure index approach is insufficient because it relies on the agent choosing to read files, which won't work for mandatory rules. A hybrid approach using frontmatter metadata (inject: always vs inject: index) would properly handle both types. Critical discovery: whether steering files are already loaded in bridge mode can be tested immediately by checking if the Telegram bot currently outputs the "ЁЯЯа цФ╢хИ░ / ЁЯЯа цЙУчоЧ" acknowledgment format.</learned>
  <completed>Completed architectural analysis and determined that pure Approach A is inadequate. Designed a hybrid injection strategy using frontmatter-driven classification. Identified implementation touchpoints in memory.ts (buildWikiIndexBlock extension, buildPreambleSections, buildPreambleWithBreakdown, PreambleBreakdown interface). Estimated implementation scope at ~40-55 lines plus smoke test, with token cost of ~150-1350 tokens per session depending on closed-loop-system handling.</completed>
  <next_steps>Waiting for user to answer the critical test question: does the Telegram bot currently output "ЁЯЯа цФ╢хИ░ / ЁЯЯа цЙУчоЧ" acknowledgment format when receiving tasks? This will determine if the steering disconnect actually exists or if bridge mode already loads steering files. If disconnect exists, user needs to decide whether closed-loop-system.md should use always or index injection (1200 token cost difference). Once decided, will create specification and delegate implementation to Kiro.</next_steps>
  <notes>The analysis revealed a free validation test that could eliminate the need for this entire feature if steering files are already loaded in bridge mode. The hybrid approach mirrors the existing wiki index pattern in memory.ts and respects the frozen-snapshot preamble constraint. Implementation would be concentrated in a single file with clear extension points already identified.</notes>
</summary>
